description: Fix SBAR Handover 'unexpected error' by adding a fallback in the AI service and voiceSbar controller. The issue is that ai.service.ts creates an Anthropic client at module load with process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, and when the key is not set, calling runAiOperation throws. Add a fallback that generates a structured handover from database data without AI when the API key is unavailable or the AI call fails.
steps: In backend/src/services/ai.service.ts, modify runAiOperation to: (a) check if process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set before attempting the API call, (b) if not set, skip the Anthropic call entirely and return a message indicating the AI service is not configured, (c) also handle the case where the Anthropic client was initialized with an empty/undefined key (move client creation inside the function or guard it). The key change: only instantiate/call Anthropic when the API key exists.

In backend/src/controllers/voiceSbar.controller.ts generateSbarHandover function, add a fallback: if runAiOperation returns the 'AI service not configured' fallback message (or throws), construct a structured SBAR handover directly from the database data that was already queried (notes, incidents, missed meds, tasks, wellbeing). Build situation/background/assessment/recommendation sections from the raw data without AI. This ensures the handover page works even without an Anthropic API key.

In the SbarHandover.tsx frontend, add error handling for the generate mutation: if the mutation fails, show the error message from the server. Also handle the case where the response contains fallback data gracefully (it should already work since the response format is the same).
acceptance_criteria: SBAR handover generation does not crash when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is not set
When AI is unavailable, a structured handover is still generated from database data
The generated fallback handover has all 4 SBAR sections populated with relevant data
Frontend shows any error gracefully without 'unexpected error' crash
verification: Run 'cd /projects/sandbox/jubilant-waddle/backend && npm run build' - should succeed
Run 'cd /projects/sandbox/jubilant-waddle/frontend && npm run build' - should succeed
